css.title: CSS Styling
css.description: Next.js supports multiple CSS approaches. Tailwind CSS v4 is the recommended default, with CSS Modules and global CSS also available.
css.tailwindV4Desc: Tailwind v4 uses native CSS @import instead of tailwind.config.js. This project already uses it.
css.globalCssText: Import global CSS in your root layout. It applies to all routes.
css.tailwindV4ChangesText: No more tailwind.config.js. Configuration is done via CSS with @theme and @custom-variant directives.
css.cssModulesScopingText: CSS Modules auto-scope class names to prevent conflicts. Use .module.css extension to enable them.
css.cssVariablesText: This project uses CSS custom properties (oklch colors) for theming. Tailwind maps them via @theme inline.
css.noRuntimeCssInJsText: Server Components do not support runtime CSS-in-JS libraries like styled-components. Use Tailwind or CSS Modules instead.

images.title: Image Optimization
images.description: The next/image component extends HTML img with automatic optimization, lazy loading, and responsive sizing.
images.liveExampleDesc: The Next.js logo below uses the Image component from next/image.
images.fillModeText: Use fill when you don't know the image dimensions. The parent must have relative positioning.
images.autoOptimizationText: Images are automatically served in modern formats (WebP/AVIF), resized, and cached at the edge.
images.lazyLoadingText: Images are lazy loaded by default. Use priority prop for above-the-fold images (LCP) to disable lazy loading.
images.widthHeightText: Always provide width/height to prevent layout shift (CLS). Use fill mode when dimensions are unknown.
images.remotePatternsText: External image domains must be explicitly allowed in next.config.ts via the remotePatterns configuration.

fonts.title: Font Optimization
fonts.description: Next.js automatically self-hosts Google Fonts with zero layout shift. This project uses Geist Sans and Geist Mono.
fonts.fontSetupDesc: Geist fonts are loaded in the root layout and applied via CSS variables.
fonts.fontSample: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
fonts.localFontsText: Use next/font/local for custom font files stored in your project.
fonts.zeroLayoutShiftText: Fonts are self-hosted at build time using size-adjust, eliminating flash of unstyled text (FOUT) and CLS.
fonts.cssVariablesApproachText: Use the variable option to create a CSS custom property, then reference it in Tailwind or your CSS.
fonts.autoSubsettingText: Google Fonts are automatically subset to only include the characters needed for your specified subsets.
fonts.noExternalRequestsText: Font files are downloaded at build time and served from your domain. No requests to Google at runtime.

metadata.title: Metadata & SEO
metadata.description: Next.js provides a Metadata API to define page metadata for improved SEO and social sharing.
metadata.staticMetadataDesc: Export a metadata object from any layout.tsx or page.tsx.
metadata.dynamicMetadataText: Use generateMetadata for dynamic pages that need data fetching.
metadata.autoMergingText: Metadata is merged from layout to page. Child metadata overrides parent fields, so you can set defaults in layouts.
metadata.deduplicationText: Next.js automatically deduplicates meta tags. Only the deepest metadata for each field is rendered in the HTML head.
metadata.openGraphText: Set openGraph in metadata for rich social media previews. Place opengraph-image.png in any route for auto OG images.
metadata.jsonLdText: For structured data, render a script tag with type="application/ld+json" in your page component body. Not part of the Metadata API.

animations.title: Animations
animations.description: CSS transitions and keyframe animations work great with Next.js. The motion library is also available for more complex animations.
animations.liveDemosDesc: Hover and observe these CSS-powered animations.
animations.motionLibraryText: The motion library (installed in this project) provides a declarative API for complex animations. It must be used in Client Components.
animations.cssFirstText: Prefer CSS transitions and keyframes for simple animations. They work in Server Components and have zero JS overhead.
animations.clientComponentsText: Animation libraries like motion require "use client" since they use React state and effects internally.
animations.reduceMotionText: Always respect prefers-reduced-motion. Use Tailwind's motion-safe: and motion-reduce: variants.
animations.layoutAnimationsText: For route transition animations, use CSS view transitions or the motion library with layout animations.
animations.fadeInText: This content faded in with a CSS keyframe animation.
animations.accessibilityText: Use Tailwind's motion variants to respect user preferences:

themes.title: Theming
themes.description: This project uses next-themes for dark mode support with CSS variables for color tokens.
themes.liveSwitcherDesc: Toggle between themes and see CSS variables update in real time.
themes.noFlashText: next-themes injects a script before hydration to set the correct class, preventing a flash of the wrong theme.
themes.systemPreferenceText: With enableSystem, the theme automatically follows the OS preference. resolvedTheme gives the actual active theme.
themes.cssVariablesStrategyText: Define colors as CSS variables in :root and .dark. Components reference variables, making theme changes instant.
themes.serverComponentsText: useTheme only works in Client Components. Server Components render with CSS variables that resolve at paint time.
themes.cardText: This card uses CSS variables that automatically adjust when the theme changes. No JavaScript re-render needed for colors.
